ontology.foodex2_description: Tacos — composite dish with meat filling in corn tortilla
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to pork tacos; A0CKW (composite meat-filled tortilla dish) used as closest defensible analogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for pork tacos as a composite dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of pork tacos consists of 2 corn tortilla shells filled with seasoned pork, lettuce, tomato, onion, and sour cream, weighing approximately 200 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Pork Tacos — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Pork Tacos (cr_id F05020087) with confidence 0.95. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record without modification.

gi_source.title: Corn tortilla GI — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to pork tacos
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for pork tacos as a composite dish. GI estimated at 52 (low) based on corn tortilla GI of approximately 52 per the University of Sydney GI Database. The pork, lettuce, tomato, onion, and sour cream components contribute negligible additional carbohydrate and are expected to lower the effective GI of the composite dish. Glycemic load per 200 g serving calculated as: (52 × 12.77 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 2) / 100 = 13.3 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 207 mg/100 g is in the borderline range (200–400 mg/100 g); estimated potassium ~280 mg/100 g provides partial offset.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit pork taco portions to one serving (2 tacos), avoid adding extra salt or high-sodium salsas, and request reduced sour cream to keep total sodium intake within daily targets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate at 12.77 g/100 g is moderate; GI estimated at 52 (low) for corn tortilla base; high protein (13.62 g/100 g) moderates postprandial glucose response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should prefer corn tortillas over flour tortillas for lower GI, limit to 2 tacos per sitting to manage total carbohydrate load, and avoid sweetened salsas or BBQ-style sauces that add sugar.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat estimated at ~3.5 g/100 g from pork and sour cream; trans fat trace at 0.1 g/100 g; sodium at 207 mg/100 g is borderline.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit pork taco consumption to one serving, choose lean pork cuts, substitute sour cream with avocado or plain yogurt to reduce saturated fat, and avoid high-sodium condiments.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork contains arachidonic acid which amplifies inflammatory pathways; trace trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) is pro-inflammatory.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should consider substituting pork with chicken or fish to reduce arachidonic acid load, monitor tolerance to nightshade ingredients (tomato, chili), and add anti-inflammatory vegetables such as avocado or cabbage.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pork tacos are a Mexican dish consisting of corn tortilla shells filled with seasoned pork meat, shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, onions, and sour cream. The corn tortillas are typically warmed or lightly toasted, and the pork filling is seasoned with spices such as cumin, chili powder, garlic, and oregano before being pan-cooked or grilled. The dish delivers a satisfying combination of savoury, mildly spiced pork with the fresh crunch of lettuce and tomato, balanced by the cool creaminess of sour cream. Nutritionally, pork tacos provide 196 kcal per 100 g, with a high protein content of 13.62 g/100 g that supports satiety and muscle maintenance. Carbohydrate content is moderate at 12.77 g/100 g, primarily from the corn tortilla, with a low GI of approximately 52, making the glycemic impact manageable. Fat is 9.6 g/100 g with estimated saturated fat of ~3.5 g/100 g. Sodium at 207 mg/100 g is borderline and warrants attention for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Dietary fibre at 1.91 g/100 g provides modest glycemic buffering. The dish is gluten-free when made with corn tortillas and is suitable for most adults in moderate portions, though individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should consider substituting pork with leaner, less pro-inflammatory proteins.
common_user_queries: How many calories in pork tacos?
Are pork tacos high in sodium?
What is the GI of pork tacos?
Are pork tacos suitable for hypertension?
How much protein in pork tacos?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05020087) with a reported confidence of 0.95. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using corn tortilla as the primary carbohydrate vehicle. Saturated fat and potassium are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for comparable pork taco compositions and are clearly flagged.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite pork taco preparation is not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as a direct match. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the HPB source for this record. calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient data not reported in the HPB source for this composite dish. trans_fat_g set to 0.1 based on estimated trace trans fat from pork and sour cream components per USDA FoodData Central analogous data. saturated_fat_g estimated at 3.5 g/100 g and potassium_mg estimated at 280 mg/100 g from USDA FoodData Central data for comparable pork taco preparations; these are estimates and not directly reported by HPB. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on corn tortilla GI from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for pork tacos as a composite dish was found. Occasion tag 'breakfast' from input retained in meal_context as 'casual dining' but reclassified to lunch/dinner as primary meal context, as pork tacos are not conventionally a breakfast food. Pork is not halal-certified; is_halal set to false.
